guide.title: How to invest in precious metals
guide.subtitle: There are multiple ways to gain exposure to gold, silver and platinum, each with its own risk, cost and complexity. This guide compares all options so you can choose the one that best fits your profile.
guide.quickComparisonDesc: Summary of the main precious metals investment methods.
guide.detailedAnalysisDesc: Pros, cons, accessibility level and estimated cost for each option.
guide.etfTitle: Top ETFs and ETCs in Europe
guide.etfDesc: Physically-backed exchange-traded products available through European brokers. Filter by metal and sort by any column.
guide.etfDisclaimer: Indicative data. TER and fund size may vary. Check the product factsheet before investing.
guide.legalText: This guide is for informational and educational purposes only. It does not constitute financial, tax or investment advice. ETF data is indicative and may not be up to date. Before making any investment decision, consult a qualified financial advisor. Metalorix is not responsible for investment decisions made based on this information.

ratioPage.title: Gold/Silver Ratio today
ratioPage.subtitle: The gold/silver ratio shows how many ounces of silver are needed to buy one ounce of gold. It is one of the oldest and most widely followed metrics among precious metals investors.
ratioPage.whatIsP1: The gold/silver ratio is simply the price of gold divided by the price of silver. If gold is $2,000/oz and silver is $25/oz, the ratio is 80.
ratioPage.whatIsP2: Historically, this ratio has fluctuated between 15 and 120. A high ratio suggests silver is "cheap" relative to gold, while a low ratio indicates silver is "expensive" by comparison.
ratioPage.howToUse: How to use it for investing?
ratioPage.above80: Silver potentially undervalued. Historically a good time to buy silver or swap gold for silver.
ratioPage.zone6080: Normal zone. No clear signal of overvaluation or undervaluation.
ratioPage.below60: Gold potentially undervalued vs silver. Consider increasing gold exposure.

goldPricePage.title: Gold price today
goldPricePage.subtitle: Live gold (XAU/USD) price updated in real time. Interactive chart with historical data, daily change and price per ounce, gram and kilogram.
goldPricePage.whatMoves: What moves the gold price today?
goldPricePage.factor2: Inflation data (CPI) in the US and Europe

gramPricePage.title: Gold price per gram today
gramPricePage.subtitle: How much is a gram of gold? Price updated in real time in euros and dollars. We also show the price by karat for jewellery.
gramPricePage.howCalculated: How is the gold price per gram calculated?
gramPricePage.howCalculatedP1: Gold is quoted internationally in US dollars per troy ounce. One troy ounce equals 31.1035 grams. To get the price per gram, divide the ounce price by 31.1035.
gramPricePage.howCalculatedP2: To convert to euros, the current EUR/USD exchange rate is applied. The euro price fluctuates based on both the gold price and the exchange rate.
gramPricePage.karatIntro: The price shown is for pure 24-karat gold (999 fine). Jewellery gold typically has lower purity:

productsPage.title: Investment coins and bars
productsPage.subtitle: Detailed profiles of the most traded gold and silver coins and bars. Purity, fine weight, typical premium over spot, liquidity, taxation and investor profile suitability.

converterPage.title: Gold price in different currencies
converterPage.subtitle: Check the price of gold, silver and platinum in 11 international currencies. Choose between troy ounce, gram or kilogram. Exchange rates updated in real time.
converterPage.whyMatters: Why does currency matter when investing in gold?
converterPage.whyMattersP1: Gold is quoted internationally in US dollars (USD), but the real cost of your investment depends on the currency you operate in. A European investor may see gold rise in USD while it remains flat in EUR if the euro strengthens against the dollar.
converterPage.whyMattersP2: That is why it is essential to check the price in your local currency. This tool shows you the updated price in EUR, GBP, CHF, JPY, AUD, CAD, CNY, INR, MXN and BRL, per troy ounce, gram and kilogram.

comparatorPage.title: Comparator: Gold vs Silver vs Platinum
comparatorPage.subtitle: Compare the historical performance of the three main precious metals. Toggle each metal on or off, switch between percentage change and absolute price, and select your preferred time period.
comparatorPage.howToReadP1: In "% Change" mode, all metals start at 0% at the beginning of the selected period, allowing you to compare which metal has risen or fallen the most in relative terms.
comparatorPage.howToReadP2: In "Price USD" mode, the absolute price in dollars per troy ounce is shown. Since gold trades much higher than silver, this mode is useful for viewing each metal's individual price evolution.
comparatorPage.goldFactors: Safe haven, central bank reserves, inflation

calendarPage.title: Economic calendar
calendarPage.subtitle: Key macroeconomic events that directly impact gold, silver and platinum prices. From Fed decisions to inflation data.
calendarPage.whyFollowP1: Precious metals are extremely sensitive to monetary policy and macroeconomic data. A surprise Fed rate decision can move the gold price 2-3% within minutes.
calendarPage.whyFollowP2: Knowing the dates of these events allows you to anticipate periods of high volatility, adjust your positions and make more informed decisions. Events marked with "high" impact are those that have historically generated the largest moves in precious metals.

calculatorPage.title: Historical ROI calculator
calculatorPage.subtitle: How much would you have earned investing in gold, silver or platinum? Enter the amount, choose the metal and date, and calculate the real return with historical data.
calculatorPage.isItWorthP1: Gold has historically been one of the most reliable safe haven assets. Over the last 20 years, it has gone from around $400/oz to over $2,000/oz, delivering cumulative returns of over 400%.
calculatorPage.isItWorthP2: However, past performance does not guarantee future results. Gold does not generate dividends or interest, and its value depends primarily on global risk perception, inflation and central bank monetary policy.
calculatorPage.isItWorthP3: This calculator uses real historical prices from futures markets to simulate how an investment would have performed in the past.
